title: Seasonal Eye Macro Grid AI
sourceTitle: Seasonal Eye Macro Grid AI Prompt

imagePrompts.prompt: Create a reusable seasonal eye macro grid with macro eye grid with seasonal reflections, tight close-up framing, and clean panel consistency. Using an eye close-up as reference, create a 3:4 four-panel hyperrealistic eye macro, panels arranged top to bottom: Spring, Summer, Autumn, Winter. Panel 1 (Spring): The eye wears a cherry-blossom pink contact lens. Lashes are adorned with tiny spring flowers. Style: delicate and beautiful, soft lighting, pastel healing palette. Reference Eye: the same eye shape from the uploaded close-up reference. Season Panel Order: Spring, Summer, Autumn, Winter arranged top to bottom. Iris Environment: tiny seasonal landscapes reflected inside the iris while the eyelid shape stays consistent. Macro Detail: sharp lashes, wetline reflections, pores, tear film, and realistic iris fibers. Lighting Variation: fresh spring green, hot summer gold, warm autumn amber, cool winter blue. Panel Layout: 3:4 vertical four-panel strip with equal spacing and no text labels. Subject: Make the same eye shape from the uploaded close-up reference the clear hero subject. Preserve the source prompt's subject hierarchy and avoid adding unrelated people or props. Scene and Context: Place the subject in four stacked seasonal iris environments while eyelid shape, lashes, and identity cues remain consistent. Keep the background details useful to the concept rather than decorative filler. Composition and Layout: Use 3:4 vertical four-panel strip with equal spacing and no text labels. Keep the layout faithful to this template's own visual mechanic and do not introduce unrelated layout systems. Style and Rendering: Render in hyperreal eye macro photography with crisp iris fibers, tear film, eyelashes, pores, and believable reflections. Keep this template's fixed material, texture, camera, illustration, or UI finish consistent across variants. Color and Lighting: Use spring green, summer gold, autumn amber, and winter blue-violet as distinct panel atmospheres. Lighting should match the template mood, separate the hero subject from the background, and stay readable at thumbnail size. Visible Typography: English-only, Latin alphabet only. Use one concise English headline or short English labels only when the template needs visible writing; otherwise keep visible writing minimal, intentional, and clean. Quality Restrictions: Avoid random letters, watermarks, malformed hands or faces, broken anatomy, duplicate logos, noisy background clutter, unreadable microcopy, and off-style artifacts.

promptTranslations.zh:
使用以下英文图像生成提示词；保留可见文字、品牌名、变量值和参数为英文。

imagePrompts.title: Seasonal Eye Macro Grid Variant 01

imagePrompts.prompt: Create a reusable seasonal eye macro grid with macro eye grid with seasonal reflections, tight close-up framing, and clean panel consistency. Using an eye close-up as reference, create a 3:4 four-panel hyperrealistic eye macro, panels arranged top to bottom: Spring, Summer, Autumn, Winter. Panel 1 (Spring): The eye wears a cherry-blossom pink contact lens. Lashes are adorned with tiny spring flowers. Style: delicate and beautiful, soft lighting, pastel healing palette. Reference Eye: one consistent adult eye used as the identity anchor across all panels. Season Panel Order: Dawn Spring, Noon Summer, Dusk Autumn, Moonlit Winter. Iris Environment: micro weather scenes inside the iris with petals, sunlight, leaves, and snow sparkle. Macro Detail: photoreal macro texture, believable skin pores, natural catchlights, and crisp eyelashes. Lighting Variation: soft green, bright cyan-gold, copper orange, icy violet-blue. Panel Layout: clean stacked editorial panels with thin white separators. Subject: Make one consistent adult eye used as the identity anchor across all panels the clear hero subject. Preserve the source prompt's subject hierarchy and avoid adding unrelated people or props. Scene and Context: Place the subject in four stacked seasonal iris environments while eyelid shape, lashes, and identity cues remain consistent. Keep the background details useful to the concept rather than decorative filler. Composition and Layout: Use clean stacked editorial panels with thin white separators.
